name: Bodyweight Glute Bridge
cues: Drive the hips up, squeeze hard at the top
execution_steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, heels close to your glutes.
Set your feet hip-width apart and rest your arms at your sides for balance.
Brace your core and press through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top until your body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Lower your hips under control back toward the floor without fully relaxing.
good_when: You are a beginner learning to feel and activate the glutes.
You have no equipment and want a joint-friendly glute exercise at home.
You need a low-fatigue activation drill before heavier lower-body work.
bad_when: Bodyweight no longer challenges the glutes (add load or switch to a hip thrust).
You want to train the glutes in a stretched position (choose an RDL or deep squat).
